fingerprints.method_out_of_state: AHCA Clearinghouse (CHAI) at flclearinghouse.com — ORI: EDOH0380Z

seo.title: Florida CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Florida - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity florida, how to transfer CNA license to florida, florida CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Florida.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Florida requires a state-approved CNA training program. Florida also offers a unique 'Challenger Route' allowing individuals 18+ with a high school diploma to sit for the exam without formal training.

steps.title: Pass Background Screening
steps.description: All applicants must pass a Level II LiveScan background check through the AHCA Clearinghouse (ORI: EDOH0380Z).

steps.title: Register for the State Competency Exam
steps.description: Register through Prometric at prometric.com/nurseaide/fl. The exam has both a written and skills component.

steps.title: Submit Application via FL Health Source
steps.description: Apply online at flhealthsource.gov to be added to the Florida Nurse Aide Registry.

documents.detail: Certificate from your state-approved CNA program, or high school diploma/GED if using the Challenger Route.
Official results from your Prometric competency exam.
Completed via AHCA Clearinghouse (flclearinghouse.com) using ORI: EDOH0380Z.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Can I challenge the CNA exam in Florida without training?
faqs.answer: Yes. Florida's Challenger Route lets individuals 18 or older with a high school diploma take the CNA exam without completing a formal training program.

faqs.question: Who administers the Florida CNA exam?
faqs.answer: The Florida CNA competency exam is administered by Prometric. Register at prometric.com/nurseaide/fl.

faqs.question: How do I verify a Florida CNA license?
faqs.answer: Use the FL DOH MQA Provider Search at mqa-internet.doh.state.fl.us/MQASearchServices/HealthCareProviders.

faqs.question: How do I transfer my CNA to Florida?
faqs.answer: Apply for endorsement through the FL Health Source portal (flhealthsource.gov). You must hold an active out-of-state CNA certificate and pass a Level II background check.

faqs.question: How long does Florida CNA endorsement take?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications are processed in 2 to 4 weeks once your file is complete.

reciprocity.notes: Out-of-state CNAs apply for endorsement through the FL Health Source online portal. A Level II LiveScan background check is required. No state exam retake if approved.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: Information on how to become a CNA in Florida.
evidence_considered: Search volume heavily favors 'become a CNA'

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA license to Florida.
evidence_considered: Search queries heavily favor 'transfer CNA license to Florida'

hero.title: Become a CNA in Florida
hero.dek: Florida CNA certification requires a 120-hour training program, Florida Board of Nursing eligibility, and a passing score on the NNAAP (Prometric) exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Florida | Requirements & Steps
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in Florida. Learn about Level 2 Background checks, Prometric exam challenge routes, and training.
target_queries: how to become a cna in florida

new_applicant.faq_heading: Common Questions about Getting a CNA in Florida

sections.label: Basic Requirements
sections.body: Florida offers a unique 'Challenger Route' where candidates 18 or older with a high school diploma can take the state exam without completing a formal program, though passing the Level II background check is mandatory regardless of route.

faq.q: Can I challenge the CNA exam in Florida?
faq.a: Yes, Florida allows individuals 18 or older with a high school diploma to challenge the CNA exam without attending a formal CNA program.
